steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The phrase “Hoping to help her students and their families outside the classroom” is a participial phrase — it starts with a present participle and describes the subject. “Huerta left teaching to start...” is the independent clause that follows.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: An introductory participial phrase requires a comma before the independent clause. Choice C provides this comma. The period, semicolon, and missing punctuation in A, B, and D each fail to follow this rule. That matches choice C — C is correct.
